cohibitable.ex: The two roommates found the house cohibitable tonight.

cohort.def: 步兵大隊、一群人、同伴的人的人物 (單數)
cohort.ex: He arrived with a small cohort of loyal supporters tonight.

coif.def: 小帽、緊帽、給...戴小帽的人的人
coif.ex: The old woman wore a simple velvet coif on her head.

coiffeur.ex: The famed coiffeur cut the actress's hair for the party.

coiffeuse.ex: The skilled coiffeuse gave her a beautiful new style.

coiffure.ex: Her elegant coiffure was decorated with small flowers.

coil.def: 線圈、盤繞、捲盤的人的人
coil.col: ignition coil, coil up, snake coil
coil.ex: The massive heavy snake began to coil around the branch.

coiling.ex: I saw coiling smoke rising from the forest clearing.

coin.def: 硬幣、創造(新詞)、鑄造的人的人的人物 (單數)
coin.col: toss a coin, gold coin, flip a coin
coin.ex: He flipped a small gold coin to decide who would go.

coinage.def: 貨幣系統、造幣、新詞語的人物 (單數)
coinage.ex: The new coinage was introduced in the city today.

coincide.def: 同時發生、相符、重合的人的人
coincide.ex: The festival will coincide with the local holiday tonight.

coincidence.def: 巧合、同時發生的人物 (單數)
coincidence.ex: Meeting you here is a truly remarkable coincidence.

coincident.def: 同時發生的、一致的人的人物
coincident.ex: There were several coincident events in the history.

coincidentally.ex: The two teams won their games coincidentally today.

co-inheritor.ex: He is the co-inheritor of the family's large estate.

coir.ex: Tough coir fiber is used to make strong doormats.

coition/coitus.ex: The report examines the biology of human coitus.

coke.def: 焦炭、(俚)古柯鹼、可樂的人物 (單數)
coke.ex: He ordered a diet coke with his burger last night.

colander.ex: Use a metal colander to drain the hot pasta water.

cold.def: 寒冷的、感冒、冷淡的的人的人物
cold.col: catch a cold, cold feet (膽怯), in cold blood, cold war
cold.ex: The room was very cold, quiet, and truly cheerless.

cold-blooded.ex: That was a cold-blooded crime that shocked the city.

cold-hearted.ex: The manager made a cold-hearted decision to fire him.

coldly.ex: She stared at him coldly and then walked away today.

coldness.ex: The emotional coldness of the father hurt the child.

cole.def: 油菜、甘藍類植物的人物 (單數)
cole.ex: The farmer planted a large field of cole this spring.

coleslaw.ex: She served some creamy coleslaw with the fried fish.

colic.def: 絞痛、疝痛的人的人物 (單數)
colic.ex: The baby suffered from bad colic and cried all night.

coliseum/colosseum.def: 大體育館、羅馬圓形劇場人物 (單數)
coliseum/colosseum.ex: The Roman Colosseum is a famous landmark in Italy.

collaborate.def: 合作、協作、通敵的人的人
collaborate.ex: Scientists from ten nations will collaborate today.

collaboration.ex: The project is a major international collaboration.

collaborator.def: 合作者、通敵者的人物 (單數)
collaborator.ex: He has been my long-time collaborator for years.

collage.def: 拼貼畫、大雜燴的人物 (單數)
collage.ex: She made a photo collage of her summer vacation.

collapse.def: 倒塌、瓦解、崩潰、暴跌的人的人
collapse.ex: The old building began to collapse after the quake.

collapsible.ex: She carried a small collapsible umbrella in her bag.

collar.def: 衣領、項圈、抓住(罪犯)的人的人的人物 (單數)
collar.ex: The dog wore a red leather collar with its name on.

collarbone.ex: He fractured his collarbone falling off his bike.

collate.def: 核對、校勘、整理(文件)的人的人
collate.ex: We need to collate all the reports before meeting.

collateral.def: 擔保品、抵押品、附帶的人物 (單數)
collateral.col: collateral damage (附帶損害), put up collateral
collateral.ex: The bank required some collateral for the large loan.

collation.def: 校勘、整理、便餐的人物 (單數)
collation.ex: A light collation was served at the end of party.

colleague.ex: He discussed the problem with his former colleague.

collect.def: 收集、採集、募捐、人的人的人物 (收)
collect.col: collect data, collect stamps, collect thoughts
collect.ex: She likes to collect rare stamps from other nations.

collected.def: 鎮定的、收集的人的人 (或物)
collected.ex: He remained calm and collected during the crisis.

collectively.ex: The group decided collectively to proceed with plan.

collector.def: 收藏家、收集者、收票員的人物 (單數)
collector.col: tax collector, art collector, ticket collector
collector.ex: The local tax collector visited the shop yesterday.

college.def: 大學、學院、學會的人物 (單數)
college.col: go to college, college student, electoral college
college.ex: She is planning to go to college after she finishes.

collegiate.def: 學院的人、大學生的人的人物
collegiate.ex: He enjoyed participating in collegiate sports today.

collide.def: 碰撞、衝突、抵觸的人的人
collide.ex: The two cars managed to collide at the junction.

collie.ex: The border collie was rounding up the young sheep.

collier.def: 煤礦工人、運煤船人物 (單數)
collier.ex: The local collier worked in the mines for thirty years.

colliery.def: 煤礦坑、煤礦場人物 (單數)
colliery.ex: The abandoned colliery was dangerous and quiet.

collinear.def: 共線的、在同一直線上的人的人物
collinear.ex: These three points are collinear on the local map.

collision.ex: The two big trucks had a serious head-on collision.

colloquial.def: 口語的、會話的人的人物
colloquial.ex: He used a lot of colloquial expressions in speech.

colloquially.ex: The new park is known colloquially as 'the green'.

collude.ex: They tried to collude with investigators tonight.

collusion.ex: There was a secret collusion between the rivals.

cologne.ex: He wore a discrete smell of expensive cologne today.

colon.def: 冒號(:)、(解)結腸、哥倫(幣)的人物 (單數)
colon.ex: Put a colon at the end of the sentence to show list.

colonel.ex: He was promoted to colonel for his great loyalty.
